name: Michael M Noyes PC
description_short: Colorado bankruptcy attorney and CPA offering Chapter 7, 11, and 13 bankruptcy filing, tax relief, and foreclosure defense since 1986.
description_long:
Michael M. Noyes PC is a Denver-based law firm founded in 1986 specializing in bankruptcy, tax, and real estate matters. Michael M. Noyes is a licensed Colorado Attorney at Law, a Colorado Certified Public Accountant, and holds Board Certification as a Consumer Bankruptcy Attorney from the American Bankruptcy Board of Certification. The firm is registered as a debt-relief agency under the Bankruptcy Code.

The firm provides comprehensive bankruptcy services across all three chapters: Chapter 7 (liquidation), Chapter 11 (business reorganization), and Chapter 13 (wage earner repayment plans). They also handle IRS and tax problem resolution, including payment plan negotiation, lien and levy relief, and foreclosure defense to help clients save their homes. The website emphasizes that tax problems may have multiple solutions requiring individualized case analysis.

Noyes Law distinguishes itself through the dual expertise of having both attorney and CPA credentials, enabling integrated analysis of tax and financial issues within bankruptcy cases. The firm has maintained continuous practice for nearly 40 years, suggesting established client relationships and institutional knowledge. Their focus on explaining different bankruptcy chapter options and tax alternatives suggests client education is part of their approach.

The firm appears to be a solo or small practice with a physical office in Denver's Centerra Building. While the website provides clear service descriptions and contact information, there is limited information about case outcomes, client reviews, or specific success metrics. Prospective clients should note that this is a specialized legal practice requiring in-person consultation to discuss individual circumstances.
pros: Board certified in Consumer Bankruptcy by American Bankruptcy Board of Certification
Dual credentials as both Colorado licensed attorney and CPA, enabling integrated tax and bankruptcy analysis
39+ years of continuous practice since 1986 in bankruptcy, tax, and real estate matters
Registered debt-relief agency compliant with Bankruptcy Code requirements
Offers all three bankruptcy chapter options (7, 11, and 13) plus tax and foreclosure services
Provides specific guidance on IRS payment plans as alternative to liens and wage levies
Clear explanation that bankruptcy may discharge unsecured debt while addressing non-dischargeable obligations
cons: Solo or very small practice with limited information about staffing or backup attorney coverage
No client testimonials, case results, or success metrics published on website
No pricing information, fee structures, or payment plan options disclosed
Limited online presence beyond basic website; no visible social media engagement or recent blog updates
Website requires in-person consultation for case evaluation with no virtual consultation option mentioned
best_for: Colorado residents facing bankruptcy with complex tax issues who benefit from attorney-CPA coordination
Homeowners in foreclosure seeking legal defense strategies combined with financial restructuring
Individuals with significant IRS debt seeking alternatives to liens and wage garnishments
diagnosis: Michael M. Noyes PC is best for Colorado residents with significant financial distress involving both bankruptcy and tax complications who value the integrated perspective of an attorney-CPA team. The primary caveat is that this is a specialized legal practice requiring in-person consultation, with limited published information about outcomes or pricing—prospective clients should call (303) 756-6789 to discuss their specific situation.
services: Chapter 11 Bankruptcy filing for business reorganization
Chapter 13 Bankruptcy filing and wage earner plan administration
IRS tax problem resolution and negotiation
Tax payment plan setup and negotiation
IRS lien and levy relief strategies
Debt relief agency services under Bankruptcy Code
Individual financial consultation and case evaluation
